subject: Databases
title: Disk I/O Is the Bottleneck
description: A quick win: understand Disk I/O Is the Bottleneck.

hook.text: A CPU processes data in nanoseconds. Reading from an SSD takes microseconds. That's 1,000x slower.

buildup.text: Databases spend most of their time waiting for disk reads, not computing. I/O dominates query time.

discovery.text: The buffer pool caches hot data in RAM. Cache hits avoid disk entirely. Cold data forces slow reads.

twist.text: An index reduces I/O by reading only the relevant pages. Fewer pages read means faster queries.

climax.text: SSD replaced spinning disks and cut random I/O time by 100x. But memory is still 100x faster than SSD.

punchline.text: Performance is about reducing disk reads. Everything else is secondary.

quiz.question: Why is disk I/O the main bottleneck for database queries?
options: Disk reads are orders of magnitude slower than CPU processing or memory access
Disks can only read one byte at a time
Modern CPUs are too slow to process query results
